narrativeOwner: Prof. Dezhen Song, VP Graduate Education & Prof. Hao Li, Dean of UG Division

assessment.headline: Graduate applications have crossed 9,500 with strong growth in Machine Learning and Applied AI programs. Quality signals are encouraging — 13% of applicants come from Top 100 institutions, up from 12% last cycle. But the conversion bottleneck is real: nearly half of all applications are sitting with faculty for review, and offer deadlines are days away.
whereWeStand: 520 offers issued so far with 340 accepted — 65% yield rate, tracking ahead of AY 2025-26 at the same point. Target: 400 accepted by April.
Application volume is tracking ahead of last cycle at 9,508. Machine Learning remains the dominant program (2,180 MSc + 680 PhD applications), followed by Computer Science.

The faculty review backlog (4,878 applications) is the critical constraint. If reviews don't accelerate before deadlines, MBZUAI risks slow conversion against competing universities with faster turnaround.
Top 100 institution applicants are up to 1,240 with 128 accepted — these are the highest-value candidates and need priority processing.

Notable admits: Vikram Krishnamurthy (IIT Bombay, ICML 2025 publication) chose MBZUAI over ETH Zurich. Amira Hassan (EMNLP first-author, Google PhD Fellowship finalist) has a pending offer — decision expected March 15.
PhD admits are skewing heavily toward ML — Computational Biology and Robotics representation remains below target and needs recruitment attention.
whatsNext: Graduate application deadlines are imminent (Feb 27–28 for CB, HCI, MAAI, UGRIP). The faculty review backlog must clear before offers can be made. **Deadline: Feb 28 · Owner: VP Graduate Education**
Alsakhar full proposals due mid-April with external reviews to follow — this affects PhD admissions pipeline indirectly. **Deadline: April 15 · Owner: Director of Research Admin**
Admissions committee to prioritize Top 100 institution applicants for accelerated review starting next week. **Deadline: March 17 · Owner: Graduate Admissions Committee**

assessment.headline: Cohort 2 is forming with 101 accepted students from 850 applicants — a 91% yield rate that signals strong conversion once offers are made. But the composition isn't where it needs to be: business track at 15% (target 30%) and UAE nationals at 12% (target 20%). The gender target is being met. A bridging program for underprepared UAE nationals is in design but won't help until Cohort 3.
whereWeStand: The 91% yield rate is exceptionally high — once MBZUAI makes an offer, students almost always accept. The challenge is upstream: getting the right applicant mix.

Business track enrollment is at 15% against a 30% target, and UAE nationals at 12% against 20%. Gender target (30% female) is being met. Recruitment strategy adjustments are underway but may not close the gap for Cohort 2.
8 Sheikh Tahnoon Scholars have been awarded — these are the highest-caliber undergraduate admits and represent the program's flagship talent.

James Whitfield (IOI Silver Medal, chose MBZUAI over Oxford) and Khalid Al-Mansouri (UAE Science Olympiad Gold, Sheikh Tahnoon Scholar) headline the cohort. Priya Sharma (ADNOC Youth Innovation Winner) strengthens the business track.
whatsNext: A UG bridging program for underprepared UAE nationals is in design — 12-month pre-undergraduate, non-residential, fee-paying. This could address the UAE national shortfall starting Cohort 3. **Target: Cohort 3 · Owner: Dean of UG Division**
UG faculty recruitment is prioritizing practical relevance over pure academic prestige, with external recruitment firms engaged. **Deadline: April 30 · Owner: Provost Office**
Cohort 2 orientation planning begins in April — housing and student services capacity being confirmed. **Deadline: April 15 · Owner: Student Services**
